title: Image Attack
content:
{{Top|rwm}}
'''Image Attack''' was a feature of [[StarWars.com]], showcasing 294 "behind the scenes" images from [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. It was updated with a new image almost daily, from [[June 12]], [[2002]] to [[December 23]], [[2003]]. It was perhaps most significant for revealing the names of obscure characters from the film.

==Media==
===Installments===
{| {{Prettytable}}
! Date !! Name !! Link !! Description
|-
| [[December 23]], [[2003]]|| The Last Attack|| || Twin-[[WESTAR-34 blaster pistol/Legends|pistol]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] ([[Temuera Morrison]]) guns down [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|his foe]] while shooting the [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] landing platform fight scene.<ref name="The Last Attack">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031223.html|series=Image Attack|text=The Last Attack|date=2003-12-23}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 18]], 2003|| Mourning Larses|| || [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Whitesun]] ([[Bonnie Maree Piesse]]) and [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]] ([[Joel Edgerton]]) are quiet at [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|Shmi's]] funeral.<ref name="Mourning Larses">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031218.html|series=Image Attack|text=Mourning Larses|date=2003-12-18}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 16]], 2003|| Kenobi's Clue|| ||[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] ([[Ewan McGregor]]) holds a vital clue in a mystery that would unravel the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]].<ref name="Kenobi's Clue">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031216.html|series=Image Attack|text=Kenobi's Clue|date=2003-12-16}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 11]], 2003|| Never to Return|| ||The tragic events on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] serve to keep [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] ([[Hayden Christensen]]) from ever returning to this planet.<ref name="Never to Return">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031211.html|series=Image Attack|text=Never to Return|date=2003-12-11}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 9]], 2003|| Between Duty and Love|| ||[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] ([[Natalie Portman]]) struggles to remain the realist when confronted by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin's]] unabashed outpouring of his forbidden feelings.<ref name="Between Duty and Love">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031209.html|series=Image Attack|text=Between Duty and Love|date=2003-12-09}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 4]], 2003|| Pilot Jango Fett|| ||[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] ([[Temuera Morrison]]) deftly guides the [[Slave I/Legends|Slave I]] through a tumbling storm of asteroids.<ref name="Pilot Jango Fett">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031204.html|series=Image Attack|text=Pilot Jango Fett|date=2003-12-04}}</ref>
|-
| [[December 2]], 2003|| Forceful Advisors|| ||[[Stass Allie/Legends|Stass Allie]] ([[Lily Nyamwasa]]) and [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] ([[Matt Sloan]]) advise the highest levels of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] government, assisting Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in the management of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Crisis]].<ref name="Forceful Advisors">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/12/imageattack20031202.html|series=Image Attack|text=Forceful Advisors|date=2003-12-02}}</ref>
|-
| [[November 25]], 2003|| Women and Children First|| ||A [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] female and child flee for their lives when their nomadic village receives an unexepected visitor.<ref name="Women and Children First">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031125.html|series=Image Attack|text=Women and Children First|date=2003-11-25}}</ref>
|-
| [[November 20]], 2003|| My Dinner with Ani|| ||[[Hayden Christensen]] as Anakin awaits the shooting of the dinner scene at Padmé's lake retreat.<ref name="My Dinner with Ani">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031120.html|series=Image Attack|text=My Dinner with Ani|date=2003-11-20}}</ref>
|-
| [[November 18]], 2003|| State of the Art||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031118.html]||A gleaming modern [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], one of many crafted as a background prop in Episode II.
|-
| [[November 13]], 2003|| The Senator from Naboo||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031113.html]||Having not seen him for a decade, the young [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] (Natalie Portman) is surprised at what she finds in Anakin Skywalker.
|-
| [[November 11]], 2003|| Cian Shee||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031111.html]||Seen lurking in the shadows of the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]] is [[Cian Shee/Legends|Cian Shee]], though what she was up to, no one can say.
|-
| [[November 6]], 2003|| Jedi Business||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031106.html]||Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) searches for trouble in the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]]. In the background are several unsavory aliens, including [[Nic Whoma]], a [[Gran/Legends|Gran]] gambler and [[Ror Ithh]], a hammerheaded carouser.
|-
| [[November 4]], 2003|| Meditating Anakin||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/11/imageattack20031104.html]||Opening himself up [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) is keenly aware of his surroundings in this sequence cut from the final release of Episode II.
|-
| [[October 30]], 2003|| Jumping Jedi!||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031030.html]||A [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] extra soars through the air in reaction to [[Battle droid/Legends|unseen enemies]].
|-
| [[October 28]], 2003|| D.o.P.||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031028.html]||Director of Photography [[David Tattersall]] gathers light information on the set of [[Jango Fett's apartment]]. With him are Assistant Director James McTeigue, Key Gaffer Eddie Knight and Best Boy Moses Fotofili.
|-
| [[October 21]], 2003|| Climbing the Pillar||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031021.html]||[[Natalie Portman]], as [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], works her way up the [[Petranaki arena|execution arena]] pillar in her escape from a ravenous [[Nexu/Legends|nexu]].
|-
| [[October 14]], 2003|| Artoo Chaperone||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031014.html]||The always helpful [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] watches over Padmé Amidala (Natalie Portman) and Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) in the hold of a [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Freighter/Legends|starfreighter]].
|-
| [[October 7]], 2003|| Smile, You're On HD Camera||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031007.html]||A pair of 24-p Sony HD cameras gather the digital imagery of Episode II.
|-
| [[October 2]], 2003|| Obi-Wan Kenobi, Jedi Knight||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/10/imageattack20031002.html]||Ten years after the death of his mentor, [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] ([[Ewan McGregor]]) has become a great [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], as [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] has foreseen. One of his greatest challenges comes not from an outside threat, though, but in training a headstrong apprentice.
|-
| [[September 30]], 2003|| Ready for Action||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030930.html]||[[Hayden Christensen]], [[Samuel L. Jackson]], [[Natalie Portman]] and [[Ewan McGregor]] shoot the final moments of the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|arena battle]].
|-
| [[September 25]], 2003|| Coco Town Streets||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030925.html]||The streets of [[CoCo Town/Legends|CoCo Town]], the district where [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] is located, were shot as miniatures in natural outdoor lighting.
|-
| [[September 23]], 2003|| Jedi Advisors||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030923.html]||[[Luminara Unduli/Legends|Luminara Unduli]] ([[Mary Oyaya]]) and [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Kit Fisto]] ([[Zachariah Jensen]]) serve as Jedi advisors to Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].
|-
| [[September 18]], 2003|| Dismissing Jar Jar||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030918.html]||Natalie Portman (Padmé Amidala) and [[Ahmed Best]] ([[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]]) perform a scene in the Coruscant apartment set.
|-
| [[September 16]], 2003|| Geonosian Spire||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030916.html]||The immense miniature of the Geonosian spire, photographed in an outside lot at [[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM]].
|-
| [[September 11]], 2003|| Disarmed||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030911.html]||Ewan McGregor (Obi-Wan Kenobi) drags a "wounded" [[Leeanna Walsman]] (Zam Wesell) from the [[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Club]]. Note that the prop [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] Kenobi's carrying in this particular take has an extended blade for stuntwork.
|-
| [[September 9]], 2003|| Dug Model||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030909.html]||An orthographic render of [[Rednax/Legends|Rednax]], a [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] known to frequent Dex's Diner.
|-
| [[September 4]], 2003|| Familiar Farm Gear||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030904.html]||These fusion generator supply tanks are built to last; they'll again be seen outside the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]] during [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] days.
|-
| [[September 2]], 2003|| The New Queen||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/09/imageattack20030902.html]||The ruling structure of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] has changed faces in the decade since [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|its blockade]]; [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Jamillia/Legends|Jamillia]] ([[Ayesha Dharker]]) is now the royal leader.
|-
| [[August 28]], 2003|| Force Push||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030828.html]||This Jedi extra feels the Force flow through her as she pushes against unseen opponents.
|-
| [[August 26]], 2003|| Tumbling Jango||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030826.html]||In his scuffle with Obi-Wan Kenobi, Jango Fett is caught offguard.
|-
| [[August 19]], 2003|| Mournful Morning||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030819.html]||This stark silhouette of Padmé Amidala sets the tone for shooting a somber scene: the explosion on the Naboo delegation landing platform.
|-
| [[August 14]], 2003|| On The Run||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030814.html]||Zam Wesell (Leeanna Walsman) is just a blur as she ducks into the Outlander Club.
|-
| [[August 12]], 2003|| Wedging It Out||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030812.html]||A crew member sits in a partially completed [[Delta-7B Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Jedi starfighter]], chatting to Supervising Art Director [[Peter Russell]].
|-
| [[August 7]], 2003|| Masterful Escape||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030807.html]||[[Silas Carson]] ([[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-Adi-Mundi]]) and Samuel L. Jackson (Mace Windu) enact the dramatic escape from the [[Petranaki arena|Geonosian arena]].
|-
| [[August 5]], 2003|| Heads Are Gonna Roll||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/08/imageattack20030805.html]||These [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] picked the wrong mother to kidnap.
|-
| [[July 31]], 2003|| Control Yoke||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030731.html]||A close-up view of the controls of the [[H-type Nubian yacht/Legends|Naboo yacht]].
|-
| [[July 29]], 2003|| Outside the Outlander||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030729.html]||Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) emerges from the shadows of the Outlander into the shadows of the night.
|-
| [[July 24]], 2003|| Seat of Power||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030724.html]||Supreme Chancellor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ([[Ian McDiarmid]]) has very particular tastes when it comes to furnishings.
|-
| [[July 22]], 2003|| Sitting Pretty||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030722.html]||Natalie Portman (Padmé Amidala) sits aboard the [[H-type Nubian yacht/Legends|Naboo starship]] set, awaiting to shoot a scene wherein she and Anakin receive important holographic information.
|-
| [[July 17]], 2003|| Path Not Taken||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030717.html]||[[Ed Natividad]]'s storyboard illustration for a scene cut from the film; Obi-Wan Kenobi follows a treacherous path along a Geonosian spire.
|-
| [[July 15]], 2003|| Tarnab Extra||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030715.html]||[[Onyeth Canavar]], a [[Tarnab/Legends|Tarnab]] seen roaming the alleys of Coruscant.
|-
| [[July 11]], 2003|| A Fallen Knight||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030711.html]||Crippled by lightsaber wounds, Obi-Wan Kenobi (Ewan McGregor) collapses to the floor.
|-
| [[July 10]], 2003|| Beneath the Hood||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030710.html]||[[Mary Oyaya]] lowers her Jedi Master's hood while waiting for her next scene as Luminara Unduli.
|-
| [[July 8]], 2003|| Bedside Bodyguard||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030708.html]||At the first sign of danger, the ever-loyal [[Dormé]] ([[Rose Byrne]]) rushes to Padmé's side (Natalie Portman).
|-
| [[July 3]], 2003|| Evil Rendezvous||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030703.html]||[[Christopher Lee]] ([[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]]) and the actor who plays Darth Sidious film the meeting of the Dark Lords at the end of Episode II.
|-
| [[July 2]], 2003|| One Last Look||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030702.html]||As Dooku escapes [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]], he takes one last look at his [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|malevolent handiwork]].
|-
| [[July 1]], 2003|| Escape from the Cauldron||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/07/imageattack20030701.html]||Having survived a harrowing cauldron ride through the [[Geonosis droid factories/Legends|Geonosian droid factory]], Padmé Amidala (Natalie Portman) steels herself for the next unexpected danger.
|-
| [[June 30]], 2003|| Walker Model||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030630.html]||This detailed practical [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer/Legends|AT-TE]] model was the basis of its digital incarnation used in the Clone Wars sequences.
|-
| [[June 27]], 2003|| Loss of a Padawan||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030627.html]||[[Agen Kolar/Legends|Agen Kolar]] ([[Tux Akindoyeni|Tux]]) watches sadly as his young Padawan, [[Tan Yuster/Legends|Tan Yuster]], dies during the Geonosian arena battle.
|-
| [[June 26]], 2003|| Conman at Work||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030626.html]||Aside from his role as [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], [[Anthony Daniels]] also plays [[Dannl Faytonni/Legends|Lt. Dannl Faytonni]], a conman seen in the Outlander Club. In between takes, he talks with the extra playing the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Ayy Vida/Legends|Ayy Vida]].
|-
| [[June 25]], 2003|| Shadowy Brothers||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030625.html]||A pair of [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] refugees lurk in the shadows of the [[Jendirian Valley/Legends|Coruscant freighter]].
|-
| [[June 24]], 2003|| Arena Casualties||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030624.html]||The sandy floor of the Geonosian execution arena is littered with dead Jedi.
|-
| [[June 23]], 2003|| Weapon of a Jedi Knight||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030623.html]||After losing his lightsaber in a battle with Darth Maul, Obi-Wan Kenobi crafted a duplicate weapon, which he almost loses here in a battle with Jango Fett.
|-
| [[June 20]], 2003|| Pillar Padmé||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030620.html]||Natalie Portman has a lofty vantage point over the minimal Geonosian arena set.
|-
| [[June 19]], 2003|| Holier Than Thou||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030619.html]||The [[Maxiron Agolerga/Legends|Naboo holyman]] that secretly marries Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala.
|-
| [[June 18]], 2003|| A Wounded Knight||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030618.html]||Obi-Wan Kenobi (Ewan McGregor), crippled by his wounds, lays at Anakin Skywalker's feet.
|-
| [[June 17]], 2003|| Jedi Office Space||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030617.html]||A concept maquette to help establish the environment of [[Mace Windu's quarters|Mace Windu's office]]. Tiny cut-outs of Mace, Yoda and Obi-Wan help establish the scale of the set.
|-
| [[June 13]], 2003|| Faithful Servant||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030613.html]||[[Rose Byrne]] plays [[Dormé]], Senator Padmé Amidala's faithful handmaiden.
|-
| [[June 12]], 2003|| Piece of Cake||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030612.html]||No one knows just how old the pastries at [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Dex's Diner]] are.
|-
| [[June 11]], 2003|| Hardcell-Class||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030611.html]||[[Industrial Light & Magic|ILM's]] digital model of a [[Hardcell-class interstellar transport/Legends|Hardcell-class Techno Union starship]]. According to the effects data, this vessel is 640 feet tall.
|-
| [[June 6]], 2003|| Travel as Refugees||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030606.html]||A pair of refugees, a [[Human/Legends|human]] and a [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], contemplate their uncertain future aboard the Jendirian Valley, the [[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|AA-9 freighter]] traveling from Coruscant to Naboo.
|-
| [[June 2]], 2003|| Preparing Padmé||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/06/imageattack20030602.html]||Make-up Supervisor [[Lesley Vanderwalt]] prepares Natalie Portman for the [[Varykino/Legends|Naboo retreat]] dinner scene.
|-
| [[May 30]], 2003|| Ready for Arena Action||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030530.html]||Mary Oyaya (Luminara Unduli) strikes a combat pose during the filming of the Geonosian arena scene.
|-
| [[May 29]], 2003|| Intrigued Extras||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030529.html]||The blue-lipped [[Hayde Gofai]] can't help but notice the striking young Padawan surveying the Outlander crowd.
|-
| [[May 28]], 2003|| Jedi Advisors||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030528.html]||Jedi Masters [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] (Matt Sloan) and [[Stass Allie/Legends|Stass Allie]] ([[Lily Nyamwasa]]) serve as close advisors to Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.
|-
| [[May 27]], 2003|| Electrum Lightsaber||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030527.html]||[[Mace Windu's lightsaber/Legends|Mace Windu's Lightsaber]] uses unique crystals to refine the energy into a purple blade, and is coated with a distinctive electrum shell.
|-
| [[May 23]], 2003|| Mirialan Master and Padawan||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030523.html]||[[Mirialan/Legends|Mirialan]] Jedi warriors Luminara Unduli (Mary Oyaya) and her apprentice [[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee]] ([[Nalini Krishan]])
|-
| [[May 22]], 2003|| Receding Platform||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030522.html]||The narrow precipice upon which stand Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) and Padmé Amidala (Natalie Portman) begins to retract.
|-
| [[May 21]], 2003|| Peculiar Dream||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030521.html]||[[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] watches as C-3PO (Anthony Daniels) awakens after his head-swapping ordeal.
|-
| [[May 20]], 2003|| Indoor Downpour||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030520.html]||The [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]] landing platform set is doused with over four swimming pools' worth of water.
|-
| [[May 19]], 2003|| Duel in the Dark||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030519.html]||In the darkness of Dooku's secret hangar, a determined Anakin Skywalker (Hayden Christensen) is lit by the eerie glow of his lightsaber.
|-
| [[May 16]], 2003|| Costume P-7||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030516.html]||Designed by [[Iain McCaig]] and [[George Lucas]], Padmé's Padawan-frustrating dress was numbered P-7 by the costume department.
|-
| [[May 15]], 2003|| Arena Scramble||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030515.html]||Narrowly surviving being trampled by a [[Reek/Legends|reek]], Jango Fett (Temuera Morrison) scrambles to his feet. This image first appeared as part of the "Choices" video presentation on ''dvd.starwars.com''.
|-
| [[May 14]], 2003|| Jedi Spy||[http://www.starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030514.html]||Obi-Wan Kenobi (Ewan McGregor) uses compact [[Electrobinoculars/Legends|electrobinoculars]] to observe Separatist activity on Geonosis.
|-
| [[May 13]], 2003|| Tundra's Support||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030513.html]||Senator [[Tundra Dowmeia]] of [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] pledges his support for Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.
|-
| [[May 12]], 2003|| Flirting in the Shadows||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030512.html]||Socialite [[Sel Maa]] flirts with the scruffy [[Daro Willits]] in a private alcove of the Outlander Club.
|-
| [[May 9]], 2003|| Watchful Assassin||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030509.html]||Zam Wesell ([[Leeanna Walsman]]) peers through retractable electrobinoculars to spy her returning assassin droid.
|-
| [[May 8]], 2003|| Like Father, Like Son||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030508.html]||Jango Fett (Temuera Morrison) and his genetically identical son [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba]] ([[Daniel Logan]]) watch the execution arena proceedings with keen interest.
|-
| [[May 7]], 2003|| Talking Blue||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030507.html]||Senator [[Orn Free Taa]] ([[Matthew Rowan]]) confers with his similarly tinted aide, [[Pampy/Legends|Pampy]].
|-
| [[May 6]], 2003|| Closed For Renovations||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030506.html]||The Outlander Club's bar stands empty as it undergoes set-dressing. The colorful tubing at the central dispenser has yet to be applied.
|-
| [[May 5]], 2003|| Preparing for Departure||[http://starwars.com/episode-ii/explore/attack/2003/05/imageattack20030505.html]||Loyal handmaiden [[Dormé]] ([[Rose Byrne]]) prepares Senator Amidala's [[Padmé Amidala's wardrobe/Legends|wardrobe]] for an extended absence. The tape markers on the floor assist in blocking the scene.
|-
